title: ITT We discuss rewatching NGE
wikitext:
[]: Second time watching Evangelion             Quote Post by JinKazama » Tue Jun 07, 2005 12:48 am all in a sudden I realized it was all about nothing but the Frieudian Theory of psychoanalysis, now I'm finding myselve some more reading about it.

[]:             Quote Post by JinKazama » Tue Jun 07, 2005 12:57 am and that could be the reason why some people prefer the TV episode ending(me too), as ending Eva by one's inspection of one's inner self is really way-out. And IMO, it could explain why it is what the author try to tell, the examination of one's self and mental treatment that follows probably would be the best way to cure mental disorders, and the same rule applies to everyone else, the way most people do to solve their problems in reality is by ignoring the self and its needs, but instead they should have looked more into themselves beofore relying too much on objects/other people/things done to supress the needs of the self in the reality. I think that is what the author of Evangelion is trying to say. and what I found interesting enough, is the dreams Shinji had and the way it happened was more or less completely following the explaination for dreams in the Freudian Theory.

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Thu Jun 16, 2005 12:30 am dear god, please let me refrain from mentioning shamshel.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 16, 2005 12:40 am It's even funnier because Shamshel is a girl. Don't get too corrupted by that psychoanalysis stuff, though, otherwise you'll become another McCoy. Truly, a fate worse than unsterilized castration.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jun 16, 2005 7:24 am Is it actually really there? I heard that Anno studied psychology, but does this in itself make it likely that scenes or concepts in Evangelion are based heavily on psycho-analysis? The whole Id, Ego, Super-ego thing? Has Anno or someone at Ganinix ever mentioned thins, or is it purely a fan thing. This isn't to say it's not a good interpretation though.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 16, 2005 8:30 am Yes, it is there. There are tons of references in NGE to psychoanalysis (or just psychology in general -- I'm not really wisened enough in the stuff to know precisely what ideas were whose). A couple of examples I can pull off the top of my head are destrudo/libido and pleasure principle.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Thu Jun 16, 2005 9:44 am DatDude wrote: The whole Id, Ego, Super-ego thing?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 16, 2005 11:05 am Ryo wrote: I've heard that the children's hair color's (brown, blue and red) also represents the Id, Ego and Super-ego.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Thu Jun 16, 2005 11:09 am perhaps (to all Rei haters) the oedpius complex as well?

[]:             Quote Post by JinKazama » Sat Jun 18, 2005 1:24 am looking at Freud's approach, is like one footstep goes a mile long, but his idea was too genius and having to take the risk of leaping too far. anyway, I heard that Anno (who made eva) before he was on the eva project he had gone through a long time of depression and was given psychotherapy treatments which are mostly of Freud's stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Sat Jun 18, 2005 1:38 am Freud was completely ridiculous. From what I've read, his theories tend to rely on specific hypothetical events that actually have a good chance of not even happening, while others are just completely impractical.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sat Jun 18, 2005 2:35 am well, isnt freud still considered one of the founders of modern psychology? and i guess some of the stuff that he talked about was kinda far out, but some stuff does apply to life, correct?

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Sat Jun 18, 2005 10:10 am Reichu wrote: Ryo wrote: I've heard that the children's hair color's (brown, blue and red) also represents the Id, Ego and Super-ego.

[]: Does anybody get kinda... scared... rewatching Eva?             Quote Post by zuka » Tue Mar 29, 2011 4:24 am So I'm thinking about rewatching Eva, but it kinda leaves me feeling pretty down by the end of it. Pretty much I really enjoy the series up until Shinji tears off Touji's leg, and then you start to realise that - barring the schoolyard scene in 26 - nothing is gonna be happy anymore. Sure, you could watch EoE, but if somebody tells me that they actually find that less emotionally draining to watch than Zeruel onwards I'd be very surprised. So, anybody else kinda scared of the end of the TV series? I just don't like everybody dying...

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Mar 29, 2011 5:08 am I am more scared of the movie. That's where the HORROR SHOW really came to hell. Still do not have the guts to rewatch that thing after >10 years.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Tue Mar 29, 2011 5:19 am I have seen it at least 7 times already and EoE is still a powerful emotional rollercoaster ride. However, these days I manage to see hope in the last scene (not everyone here agrees with me in this) so I guess it's not so bad nowadays. Yes, things like the hospital scene and Asuka's end are still very painful to watch but even that has subsided a bit now. I'll admit I have used happy fanfiction as a rebound tool for EoE pains. Might not work for everyone though.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Mar 29, 2011 5:33 am BiQ wrote: I'll admit I have used happy fanfiction as a rebound tool for EoE pains. Might not work for everyone though.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Mar 29, 2011 5:57 am I've seen eva so many times I probably remember 90% of episodes shot by shot, scene by scene. Hell, I've even watched about half of the series after messing with audio options of my PC so that the dialogue track was nearly completely muted so I could just focus on sound effects, editing of them etc. It's been 2 or so years since I rewatched it all though. I haven't had time to rewatch ANY anime series in forever (excluding rewatching Macross Frontier quite a few times when I was getting into franchise), only some films...most notably Ghibli. Those never get old and Laputa just keeps getting better and better with each rewatch EoE has pretty positive message in the end so of course it's easy to morph into something ridiculously cynical. But then we've already moved to realm of fan interprepation (which Anno kinda wanted anyway)

[]:             Quote Post by zuka » Tue Mar 29, 2011 5:58 am I think I can handle everything in the original series up until around Zeruel's attack, when the show turns that relentlessly dark corner and everything starts getting veeeery heavy. I like the heaviness from previous episodes, but I like it when it's balanced out by a genetically engineered penguin. symbv, i read your description of your reaction to EoE from another thread and I think I feel the same way towards the end of the series. i think the difference is, i too think episode 26 ends in a very happy way (considering the circumstances), but it is a bit of an emotional roller coaster getting attached to all these characters knowing that they're all going to die/dissolve into LCL/other theory

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Mar 29, 2011 6:18 am I haven't seen it since shortly after my 7th rewatch, which was probably about 6 months or so after my first viewing, so, yeah, it's been a while. I'd be very curious to see how I respond to it now. I wonder if my forays into film and the other arts have gotten me to a point where I'd like/love NGE more or less. Like, I wonder if I'd find more flaws in it now, or maybe I'd find even more things to appreciate. Anyway, I'm not really worried about the emotional impact. I've already been through it and I don't think any work of art has a chance at shattering me the way NGE did again.
summary:
[]: Second time watching Evangelion             Quote Post by JinKazama » Tue Jun 07, 2005 12:48 am all in a sudden I realized it was all about nothing but the Frieudian Theory of psychoanalysis, now I'm finding myselve some more reading about it.

[]:             Quote Post by JinKazama » Tue Jun 07, 2005 12:57 am and that could be the reason why some people prefer the TV episode
